commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No narrow repair remains; the next action is maintainer review to pick the canonical endpoint-routing PR and accept or reject the runtime baseURL alias contract.; Cleared: No concrete security defect remains after the latest head adds URL-secret metadata and redaction coverage for baseURL; the endpoint-routing boundary remains a maintainer merge-risk decision. commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `models.providers.*.baseURL` as a schema-level alias for `baseUrl`, normalizes or rejects duplicate spellings, updates config metadata/docs/redaction, and adds config plus memory-search regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +77, Docs 0, Generated 0. Total +103 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main and `v2026.6.11` accept only canonical `baseUrl` in the strict provider schema while the memory embedding client reads `providerConfig.baseUrl`; the PR body also supplies a local endpoint proof for the fixed path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow repair remains; the next action is maintainer review to pick the canonical endpoint-routing PR and accept or reject the runtime `baseURL` alias contract.

target.title: fix: support baseURL alias in memory embedding provider config resolution
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When users configure `models.providers.openai.baseURL` (capital `URL`) for a local or OpenAI-compatible embedding endpoint, the strict provider schema used to reject/drop that alias instead of exposing the canonical `baseUrl` field downstream.

Fixes #93396.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts` | Accept `models.providers.*.baseURL`, normalize it to canonical `baseUrl`, delete the alias before downstream code reads the provider config, and reject configs that set both spellings. |
| `src/config/schema.hints.ts` | Walk Zod pipes while collecting schema hint paths so transformed provider schemas keep sensitive URL metadata for `models.providers.*.baseUrl` and its `baseURL` alias. |
| `src/config/schema.test.ts` | Covers `baseURL` normalization, alias deletion, duplicate-spelling rejection, and sensitive URL metadata on the alias path. |
| `src/config/schema.hints.test.ts` | Covers discovery of the `models.providers.*.baseURL` sensitive URL hint path. |
| `src/config/redact-snapshot.test.ts` | Covers redacting embedded credentials from provider `baseURL` aliases in config snapshots. |
| `packages/net-policy/src/redact-sensitive-url.ts` | Treats `models.providers.*.baseURL` as a sensitive URL config path alongside `baseUrl`. |
| `packages/net-policy/src/redact-sensitive-url.test.ts` | Covers the alias in the shared sensitive URL path predicate. |
| `src/agents/memory-search.ts` | Keeps the direct built-in memory adapter when one exists. The OpenAI memory adapter already reads canonical provider `baseUrl`, so routing should stay on the existing adapter instead of becoming an unconfigured provider. |
| `src/agents/memory-search.test.ts` | Strengthens the `baseURL` coverage so schema-normalized config must still resolve the built-in OpenAI memory adapter and its default model. |

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `models.providers.openai.baseURL` is accepted by the production schema, normalized to `baseUrl`, then used by the real OpenAI memory embedding path to call the configured `/v1/embeddings` endpoint.

**Exact commands run after fix:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.hints.test.ts src/config/redact-snapshot.test.ts packages/net-policy/src/redact-sensitive-url.test.ts
git diff --check
PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check packages/net-policy/src/redact-sensitive-url.ts packages/net-policy/src/redact-sensitive-url.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.hints.test.ts src/config/redact-snapshot.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json packages/net-policy/src/redact-sensitive-url.ts packages/net-policy/src/redact-sensitive-url.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.hints.test.ts src/config/redact-snapshot.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts src/agents/memory-search.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.hints.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
node --import tsx /Users/yangjiajun/projects/auto-pr/workspace/openclaw/outputs/issue-93396-pr-93505/verify-baseurl-endpoint.mjs
```

**After-fix evidence:**

```text
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 4.93s

git diff --check: passed
OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256
oxfmt: All matched files use the correct format.
oxlint: passed
tsgo core: passed
tsgo core-test: passed

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 7.30s

src/config/schema.test.ts: 57 passed
src/agents/memory-search.test.ts: 37 passed

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 9.41s

src/config/schema.hints.test.ts: 11 passed

pnpm check:test-types: passed
```

The endpoint proof starts a local HTTP server, parses config through `OpenClawSchema`, registers the real OpenAI memory adapter, resolves memory search config, and calls `embedBatch`.

```json
{
  "schemaDeletedBaseURLAlias": true,
  "resolvedProvider": "openai",
  "resolvedModel": "text-embedding-3-small",
  "adapterProviderId": "openai",
  "request": {
    "method": "POST",
    "url": "/v1/embeddings",
    "authorization": "Bearer <redacted>",
    "contentType": "application/json",
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small",
    "inputCount": 2
  },
  "vectors": [
    [0.1, 0.2, 0.3],
    [1.1, 1.2, 1.3]
  ]
}
```

**Observed result:** The alias is removed after schema normalization, duplicate `baseUrl`/`baseURL` config is rejected, the alias carries the same sensitive URL metadata as `baseUrl`, memory search resolves the OpenAI built-in adapter with `text-embedding-3-small`, and the real embedding request hits the configured `/v1/embeddings` route rather than falling back to an unconfigured path.

AI-assisted: built with Codex
target.author: sunlit-deng
